name: Soda Bread (Irish Whisky Soda)
recipeInstructions:
BREAD:
1. In a large bowl, combine flour, salt, and baking soda.
2. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Stir in raisins or currants (if using).
4. In a separate bowl, combine buttermilk, honey, and whiskey (or buttermilk).
5. Add these ingredients to the dry mixture; stir just until no dry spots remain.
6. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface.
7. Knead lightly for 1 minute. Too much handling will toughen the loaves, while too little will inhibit rising.
8. Divide dough into two halves and shape each into an 8-inch round.
9. Place the shaped rounds in greased 8-inch (20 cm) round cake pans.
10. Using a floured knife, cut a cross about 1/2 inch deep in each loaf.
GLAZE:
11. In a small bowl, combine whiskey and milk.
12. Brush the loaves with the glaze.
13. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (180°C) for 35 to 40 minutes, or until the loaves sound hollow when tapped on the bottom.
14. Remove from pans and let cool on wire racks.
15. Cut into wedges.
